scenario: At carrier Polaris Logistics, an engineer configures the HTTP Webhook boundary event connector template on Camunda 8.8 and sets a 30 minutes Message TTL so late courier callbacks expire in the broker before they can interrupt a user task whose SLA window is 2 hours per delivery.
question: Which value format does the boundary event webhook template require in the Message TTL property?

options.text: A FEEL number in seconds matching the regex pattern of the field, written as a plain integer such as 1800 for 30 minutes in the broker
A cron expression in the correlation group matching the pattern of the field so the broker schedules expiry inside the message subscription
An ISO-8601 duration string matching the pattern ^(PT.*|)$, written for example as PT30M for a 30 minutes time-to-live in the broker
A Java Duration literal matching the pattern of the field, written as java.time.Duration.ofMinutes(30) inside the messageTtl property value

a.text: Incorrect. The constraint pattern on messageTtl requires PT-prefixed ISO-8601 durations or an empty string, so a plain numeric seconds value such as 1800 does not match the field pattern.

b.text: Incorrect. messageTtl sits in the correlation group and represents a duration string, not a cron expression scheduled against the message subscription.

c.text: Correct. The boundary template defines messageTtl with constraints pattern ^(PT.*|)$ and the message must be an ISO-8601 duration; the field is feel: optional and the description states Time-to-live for the message in the broker.

d.text: Incorrect. The field holds a String value that matches the ISO-8601 duration pattern; the template does not accept Java Duration literals as the configured value type.

explanation: Message TTL on the boundary event webhook template is a String property in the correlation group with the pattern constraint ^(PT.*|)$, requiring an ISO-8601 duration such as PT30M or an empty value. It controls how long the broker keeps the correlated message available for the boundary event.
